scope: Survey of every DataContract / DataMember type and serializer choice on the 4 WCF hosts surfaced in T04. Verifies absence of NetDataContractSerializer, BinaryFormatter, SoapFormatter, LosFormatter, ObjectStateFormatter, TypeNameHandling, and polymorphic [KnownType] gadgets.

nodes_added.name: TdmDatabaseTools.ExportTableParameter
nodes_added.summary: 9 DataMembers, all string/short. NO KnownType, NO IExtensibleDataObject. Sql* fields carry RAW SQL on the wire by design.

nodes_added.name: TdmDatabaseTools.ImportTableParameter
nodes_added.summary: 11 DataMembers. One nested type via Dictionary<string, List<ColumnReplaceValue>>. NO KnownType, NO IExtensibleDataObject. Sql* fields carry RAW SQL on the wire by design.

nodes_added.name: TdmDatabaseTools.ColumnReplaceValue
nodes_added.summary: 3 string DataMembers. Leaf type — no further nesting.

nodes_added.name: [XmlSerializerFormat] on XmlDocument ops
nodes_added.summary: Forces XmlSerializer (strict, no polymorphism without [XmlInclude]) for all XmlDocument-carrying operations on iToolingServer and ITdmToolData.

nodes_added.name: DataContractSerializer (default)
nodes_added.summary: Default for IDatabseImportExport operations carrying ExportTableParameter / ImportTableParameter. Strict, no polymorphism via xsi:type (that is NetDataContractSerializer territory; absent here).

nodes_added.name: No NetDataContractSerializer in tree
nodes_added.summary: Grep over all .cs returned zero hits. CVE-2017-9785-class gadget chain is structurally absent.

nodes_added.name: No BinaryFormatter / SoapFormatter / LosFormatter / ObjectStateFormatter in tree (.cs)
nodes_added.summary: 39 grep hits are all in .resx (VS build-time resource serialization), not runtime WCF surface.

edges_added.note: List<ExportTableParameter> on the wire; caller-supplied SQL inside SqlSelectCommand etc.
Plus a List<ImportTableParameter> in the same operation.
Dictionary<string, List<ColumnReplaceValue>>. Deepest nesting on the surface; leaf is a 3-string POCO.

notes: T06 refutes the devil's-advocate B6 hypothesis (WCF deserialization pre-auth RCE): no NetDataContractSerializer, no polymorphic gadget surface, no [KnownType]. However, the IDatabseImportExport DataContracts carry RAW SQL strings on the wire by design — that's a separate concern about the design of the contract itself, not about deserialization. Worth a future trace for XXE specifically on the XmlDocument parameters: WCF's default XmlSerializerFormat reader is hardened, but downstream processing of the XmlDocument by the application may not be.
